summary: Binary GUID compared with strncmp instead of memcmp
impact: Session binding checks can produce false positives when GUID contains NUL bytes, weakening client-binding validation
recommendation: Use memcmp(conn->ClientGUID, sess->ClientGUID, SMB2_CLIENT_GUID_SIZE) and keep constant-time semantics where practical

summary: FSCTL_VALIDATE_NEGOTIATE_INFO compares binary GUID with strncmp
impact: Validation may incorrectly accept mismatched client GUIDs when embedded NULs truncate comparison
recommendation: Replace strncmp with memcmp for exact 16-byte comparison

summary: Pipe-share path skips share->name allocation failure check
impact: Low-memory kstrdup failure can insert share with NULL name and later crash in strcmp during lookup
recommendation: Check share->name immediately after kstrdup regardless of share type and fail fast

summary: Direct assignment of krealloc result can lose prior pointer on OOM
impact: If expansion fails after entries exist, to_destroy becomes NULL while nr_destroy remains >0, leading to NULL dereference and leaked sessions
recommendation: Use temporary pointer (tmp = krealloc(...)); only assign on success; handle OOM path explicitly

summary: Failure exit code is masked by ! command inversion
impact: Failing suites can be reported as success and return 0, invalidating CI signal
recommendation: Capture exit code without ! (or store status immediately from command invocation) and propagate non-zero

summary: Native suites execute help/list commands instead of tests
impact: Integration/security/performance suites do not validate behavior; regressions can pass undetected
recommendation: Execute real test entrypoints (remove --help/--list placeholders) and assert pass/fail outputs

summary: Script declares /bin/sh but uses bashisms and broken command substitutions
impact: Portability and correctness issues can break build/install automation across systems
recommendation: Move shebang to line 1, use bash explicitly or POSIX syntax consistently, and remove command substitutions with redirected output

summary: Quoted make flag assembly is unsafe (SC2089/SC2090)
impact: Debug flags may be passed with literal quotes or split unexpectedly, causing unreliable builds
recommendation: Use bash arrays for make arguments and avoid concatenated shell strings

summary: FSCTL_SET_REPARSE_POINT validates input but returns success without applying changes
impact: Clients may receive success while server state is unchanged, causing protocol/behavior divergence
recommendation: Return STATUS_NOT_SUPPORTED until implemented, or implement atomic replacement semantics

summary: FSCTL_CREATE_OR_GET_OBJECT_ID returns fixed all-zero object IDs
impact: Object-ID semantics are non-compliant and can cause identity collisions across files
recommendation: Generate stable per-file object IDs per SMB semantics or explicitly reject unsupported operation

impact: Potential integer-overflow hardening gap in message-size calculation
recommendation: Use checked addition before allocation/bounds comparison (e.g., helper with overflow check)

summary: DKMS paths use unsanitized PKGVER in sudo rm/cp/sed operations
impact: Operator-supplied PKGVER containing path metacharacters can target unintended locations
recommendation: Sanitize PKGVER to a strict charset (e.g., [A-Za-z0-9._-]) before filesystem operations
